channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Funcom Announces Conan Exiles: Enhanced, a Massive Free Visual & System Overhaul For the Game's 8th Anniversary
item.content:encoded:
On May 5th, Conan Exiles marks its 8th anniversary with a massive, free update on PC. Conan Exiles: Enhanced,  provides not only a massive visual overhaul, it modernizes systems, enhances performance, and uses Unreal Engine 5 to improve rendering and more.

Things have been kind of quiet on the Conan Exiles front for a while and now we know one reason why. The announcement came with a trailer showing off some polished up in-game world footage and details on what else is coming to whet everyone’s appetite. 

Conan Exiles: Enhanced was produced in partnership with Inflexion Games, the studio behind Nightingale. Inflexion had, amid restructuring, mentioned future projects and now we know one of them.

So what can we expect besides a complete visual overhaul? For the first time, there will be smooth travel between the Exiled Lands and Isle of Siptah if you own the expansion, as both maps have been unified. In multiplayer, a server can offer support for both maps simultaneously. You'll also be able to have multiple characters per account. You will be able to transfer your saves from the UE 4 version into the Enhanced Edition built in Unreal Engine 5, so you can continue where you left off.

The UI is also getting overhauled for clarity and improved navigation. With all of this, the update reduces the client size to 40 GB down from 120GB. Enhanced also adds full Steam Deck support. There are quality of life changes, including improved crafting that lets you take ingredients from storage and follower inventories.

The Unreal Engine 4 edition will remain up for those who want to keep playing it, as Conan Exiles: Legacy, but will no longer be developed. You'll have to remove your character from the server by May 4th and if you want to play you can either do it on a private server or as a single player game going forward. The Bazaar will be discontinued on PC in Legacy, so spend your Crom Coins or save them for use in Enhanced.

There’s a FAQ accompanying the announcement if you want more info on what to expect. There are "no current plans" to bring it to other platforms, but Funcom says they're considering it.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/funcom-announces-conan-exiles-enhanced-a-massive-free-visual-and-system-overhaul-for-the-games-8th-anniversary-2000137886
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
